task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a new IFC project named 'Rosewood Cottage' with standard spatial hierarchy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Outliner or Scene properties panel in Blender/Bonsai. You should see the project named 'Rosewood Cottage' and a spatial hierarchy containing Project, Site, Building, and Storey levels.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least four perimeter walls are modeled on the ground floor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 3D viewport or Outliner. There should be at least four distinct wall elements (IfcWall) forming a perimeter or enclosure on the ground floor.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of a pitched roof with at least two planes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 3D viewport for a roof structure with at least two sloped planes (a pitched roof). In the Outliner or Object properties, these elements should be identifiable as IfcRoof or IfcSlab (with a PredefinedType of 'ROOF').

task_completion.description: Verify that a valid roofing material is created and assigned to at least one roof element.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Material properties tab or Bonsai Object properties for the roof element. A material must be assigned, and its name must contain one of the following keywords: 'Tile', 'Slate', 'Shingle', 'Clay', or 'Roof'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Blender save dialog, a file manager window showing 'pitched_roof_house.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory, or the Blender window title bar displaying the correct file path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to model the building.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with Blender's 3D viewport, outliner, and BIM properties panels, rather than just a terminal window executing a script.

integrity.description: Verify that the walls and roof have actual 3D geometry.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport should display visible, solid geometry for the walls and pitched roof, not just empty objects or locators renamed to look like IFC elements.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modeled the project from scratch rather than opening a pre-made file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots over time should show the progressive addition of elements (e.g., walls first, then roof, then materials) rather than a fully completed model appearing instantly in the first few frames.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project Name must be exactly 'Rosewood Cottage'. Output file must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/pitched_roof_house.ifc'. The model must contain at least 4 IfcWall elements. The roof must have at least 2 planes and be modeled as IfcRoof or IfcSlab (PredefinedType 'ROOF'). The assigned roofing material must contain at least one of these keywords (case-insensitive): 'Tile', 'Slate', 'Shingle', 'Clay', or 'Roof'.
